title: Lebanon's most senior Shi'ite politician says no to negotiations with Israel until war stops
snippet: Lebanon's parliament speaker Nabih Berri, the most senior Shiite politician and close Hezbollah ally, told An-Nahar newspaper the priority must be 'stopping the war before any political track.' He rejected negotiations without guarantees Israel would halt attacks. Israeli forces ordered residents out of 4 more villages in southern Lebanon. Hezbollah carried out 11 operations against Israeli forces on Sunday. Ceasefire Day 18 — hostilities continue despite the ceasefire agreement extended on April 23.
tag_reason: Nabih Berri's first direct public rejection of Israel negotiations — significant as most senior Shiite leader in Lebanon.
